description: Which wave-monitoring buoy the record came from. Added when the two raw files are combined.
Timestamp of the 26.6-minute wave record; readings every 30 minutes. Queensland does not observe daylight saving so AEST is constant.
Calendar date extracted from datetime (for daily grouping).
Southern-Hemisphere season (Summer = Dec-Feb; Autumn = Mar-May; Winter = Jun-Aug; Spring = Sep-Nov).
Hour of day for diurnal grouping.
Significant wave height: mean height of the highest one-third of waves in the record.
Maximum individual wave height in the record.
Peak energy wave period (period of the most energetic waves). Long Tp = swell; short Tp = local wind-sea.
Direction the peak-period waves are coming FROM relative to true north (0 = N; 90 = E; 180 = S; 270 = W).
Sea surface temperature from a sensor in the hull of the buoy.
